highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.149 Released
highlights.summary: This release brings a detailed `/usage` breakdown showing costs by category — skills, subagents, plugins, and per-MCP-server — so you can spot what's eating your limits. The `/diff` view now supports full keyboard navigation (arrows, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, Space, Home/End), making code review much smoother without reaching for the mouse. Markdown output also renders properly, improving readability of Claude's responses.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.147 Released: Pinned Sessions & /code-review Command
highlights.summary: Pinned background sessions (Ctrl+T in `claude agents`) now stay alive when idle and survive Claude Code updates by restarting in place, giving you persistent agent contexts without losing work. The `/simplify` command has been renamed to `/code-review`, and now actively reports correctness bugs rather than just suggesting simplifications. This makes it a proper lightweight code audit tool you can run at any point in your workflow.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family. As Claude Code users can select which model powers their sessions, this new release may offer improved reasoning and coding capabilities for demanding tasks. Check the Anthropic blog for details on performance benchmarks and how to enable it in your Claude Code configuration.

highlights.title: Show HN: Spec-Driven Development Workflow for Claude Code
highlights.summary: This workflow proposes breaking AI-assisted coding into two dimensions: first generating layered specs (requirements → code analysis → design), then splitting implementation into focused subtasks handled one at a time. This approach helps Claude Code stay on track for complex features by giving it narrow, well-defined context at each step. If Claude tends to drift or over-engineer your tasks, adopting a spec-first habit before writing any code can dramatically improve output quality.

highlights.title: Classic Article: Lessons from Building Claude Code — Prompt Caching Is Everything
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video summarizes Anthropic's own retrospective on building Claude Code, with the key insight that prompt caching is the single most impactful optimization for agentic coding workflows. By caching repeated context (system prompts, large files, CLAUDE.md instructions), you dramatically reduce latency and cost across long sessions. If you haven't structured your CLAUDE.md or system prompts with caching in mind, this is a must-watch to understand why it matters.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises to take you from zero to a solid working knowledge of Claude Code in just 20 minutes, making it one of the most time-efficient starting points for new users. It likely covers installation, basic prompting, file editing, and running commands through the agent. Bookmark this as your recommended first watch before diving into more advanced workflows.

highlights.title: Session Amnesia: The Hidden Cost of Stateless AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: This article explores a pain point many Claude Code users hit quickly: the AI forgets decisions, patterns, and context between sessions, forcing you to re-explain your codebase repeatedly. The piece frames this as 'session amnesia' and discusses strategies like persistent memory files and structured context documents to work around it. For Claude Code users, this is a strong argument for investing time in a well-written CLAUDE.md file and memory MCP tools.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/usage` in Claude Code v2.1.149+ to audit which components (skills, subagents, MCP servers) are consuming the most of your limits — then optimize or disable the heaviest ones for routine tasks.
Write your CLAUDE.md with stable, frequently-reused context at the top (project structure, coding conventions, key file paths) so prompt caching kicks in early and keeps your session costs low.

Before handing a complex feature to Claude Code, spend 5 minutes writing a short spec file: list the requirements, constraints, and expected file changes. Drop it in your project and reference it in your prompt — you'll get dramatically more focused output with fewer correction loops.
